reasoning: The September 2025 FOMC meeting resulted in a 25 basis point rate cut, fulfilling the first condition ('cut'). However, as of mid-to-late October 2025, market pricing (CME FedWatch) indicates a 94% to 100% probability of another rate cut at the October 28-29 meeting, driven by weak labor data and economic headwinds. Furthermore, the September dot plot and Fed minutes showed the median FOMC participant expected two additional rate cuts by the end of 2025. Given the overwhelming likelihood of a rate cut in October (which would break the 'cut-pause-pause' sequence) and expectations for further easing in December, the probability of back-to-back pauses in October and December is vanishingly small. I am assigning the minimum allowable probability of 0.05.
